before_model.pattern: Fat controller: auth check + idempotency + INSERT Conversation + send first SMS — all in one method, no transaction boundary
before_model.atomicity: NONE — INSERT Conversation (step 9) commits BEFORE CreateAndSendConversationMessage (step 10). Orphaned Conversation possible if SMS send fails.
before_model.tenant_tracking: CustomerId NOT stored on Conversations table — only reachable via JOIN to ConversationPhoneNumbers.CustomerId
before_model.creator_tracking: CreatedByUserId NOT persisted — workContext.CurrentUserId used at runtime only
before_model.participant_model: No ConversationParticipants table — creator tracked via profileId passed to SendConversationMessage (no ownership record)
before_model.sms_coupling: Conversation creation is COUPLED with first SMS send — cannot create conversation without sending a message

after_model.pattern: Vertical slice: CreateConversationCommand → CreateConversationHandler → atomic INSERT Conversation + INSERT ConversationParticipant(Owner)
after_model.atomicity: IDbSession.ExecuteInTransactionAsync — commit on success, rollback on any step failure. INV_C04 enforced.
after_model.tenant_tracking: CustomerId stored directly on Conversations table — sourced from ICurrentUser.CustomerId at creation time
after_model.creator_tracking: CreatedByUserId stored on Conversations table — sourced from ICurrentUser.UserId
after_model.participant_model: New ConversationParticipants table: {Id, ConversationId, UserId, Role}. Creator inserted as Owner atomically.
after_model.sms_coupling: DECOUPLED — CreateConversation does NOT send SMS. Sending is a separate feature (future slice: SendConversationMessage).
after_model.auth_model: ICurrentUser.CustomerId used for tenant scoping. UserId used for participant ownership. No profile-intersection check in handler — simplified to direct CustomerId check.

new_columns.name: CustomerId
new_columns.note: Source: ICurrentUser.CustomerId. Enables direct tenant filtering without JOIN.

columns.name: Id
columns.type: INT IDENTITY(1,1) NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY

columns.name: ProfileId
columns.note: DD_C07: authorization is profile-based — binds participant to profile that has ConversationPhoneNumber access

constraints: UNIQUE (ConversationId, UserId, ProfileId) — one row per user+profile per conversation

design_decisions.decision: CustomerId stored directly on Conversations table
design_decisions.rationale: Layer 0 requires JOIN to ConversationPhoneNumbers to resolve CustomerId — fragile, join-dependent. Direct column enables WHERE CustomerId = @CustomerId filtering on all queries without JOIN.
design_decisions.alternative_rejected: Keep JOIN-based resolution — rejected because tenant queries become JOIN-dependent (violates green-ai tenant isolation pattern)

design_decisions.rationale: Layer 0 does not persist creator — only passes profileId at message-send time. Storing UserId enables ownership checks, audit, and future participant management without runtime workContext.
design_decisions.alternative_rejected: Runtime-only — rejected because state cannot be verified post-creation

design_decisions.decision: New ConversationParticipants table with Role enum
design_decisions.rationale: Layer 0 has no participant model — creator is only implicitly linked via phone number profile mapping. Explicit participant table enforces INV_C02 (creator always Owner) and prepares for multi-participant conversations.

design_decisions.decision: IDbSession.ExecuteInTransactionAsync wraps INSERT Conversation + INSERT ConversationParticipant
design_decisions.rationale: Layer 0 has no transaction — orphaned Conversation possible. Same pattern as user_onboarding INV_003. If participant INSERT fails → rollback Conversation INSERT.
design_decisions.invariant_enforced: INV_C04 — no Conversation without at least one participant

design_decisions.decision: SMS send DECOUPLED from CreateConversation slice
design_decisions.rationale: Layer 0 couples Conversation creation with first SMS send — makes handler non-atomic (external call inside transaction boundary). In green-ai: CreateConversation creates the DB record only. SMS send is a separate command (future slice).
design_decisions.scope_note: This slice: CreateConversationHandler only. No SendConversationMessage in scope.

design_decisions.decision: Idempotency returns CONVERSATION_EXISTS error code (not silent reuse)
design_decisions.rationale: Layer 0 silently sends a reply to existing conversation — caller cannot distinguish create vs reuse. Green-ai: return Result.Failure(CONVERSATION_EXISTS) with existing ConversationId. Caller decides what to do.

design_decisions.decision: ConversationParticipants must include ProfileId — not just UserId
design_decisions.rationale: Layer 0 authorization is profile-based (via ConversationPhoneNumberProfileMappings). Binding participant only to UserId loses the profile→phone_number access context. ProfileId binds participant to the specific profile that holds phone number access — preventing cross-customer bugs and enforcing RULE_CC_002.
design_decisions.invariant_enforced: RULE_CC_002 — auth: profile must have phone number access

simplifications.note: CustomerId comes from ICurrentUser directly — no need to resolve via phone number. ConversationPhoneNumberId still stored on Conversation for future message routing, but CustomerId is independent.

simplifications.simplification: No partner name resolution in this slice
simplifications.note: Layer 0 calls _conversationPartnerFinder.GetPartnerName — external lookup. Excluded from CreateConversation scope. PartnerName passed by caller or defaulted to empty.

invariants_enforced.INV_C01: CustomerId stored on Conversations — WHERE CustomerId = @CustomerId enforced on all queries
invariants_enforced.INV_C02: INSERT ConversationParticipants(Role=Owner, UserId=CreatedByUserId, ProfileId=CallerProfileId) in same transaction as INSERT Conversations
invariants_enforced.INV_C03: Before INSERT: validate UserId (from ICurrentUser) belongs to Conversation.CustomerId — Users.CustomerId check
invariants_enforced.INV_C04: IDbSession.ExecuteInTransactionAsync — rollback both INSERTs if participant INSERT fails
invariants_enforced.RULE_CC_002: ProfileId stored on participant row — links participant to profile that has ConversationPhoneNumber access (enforced at participant INSERT)

target_handler.name: CreateConversationHandler
target_handler.command: CreateConversationCommand { ConversationPhoneNumberId, PartnerPhoneCode, PartnerPhoneNumber, PartnerName?, ProfileId }
steps: 1. Idempotency check: SELECT FROM Conversations WHERE ConversationPhoneNumberId=@id AND PartnerPhoneCode=@code AND PartnerPhoneNumber=@num AND CustomerId=@cid → if found: return Failure(CONVERSATION_EXISTS, ConversationId=existing.Id)
3. Validate ProfileId belongs to ICurrentUser via ProfileUserMappings — return Failure(INVALID_PROFILE) if not

4. ExecuteInTransactionAsync: INSERT Conversations(CustomerId, CreatedByUserId, CreatedAt, ...) → INSERT ConversationParticipants(ConversationId=new.Id, UserId=CreatedByUserId, ProfileId=@ProfileId, Role=Owner)

migration.note: Version number to be assigned. Contains: ALTER TABLE Conversations ADD CustomerId/CreatedByUserId/CreatedAt + CREATE TABLE ConversationParticipants
